notes.text: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with chronic kidney disease stage 3. Recent eGFR remains in the low 40s. Continue renal monitoring, avoid nephrotoxic medications, maintain ACE inhibitor therapy, and continue diabetes management with primary care.
Active problems reviewed today include Type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and chronic kidney disease. Patient reports no hypoglycemia and checks home glucose intermittently.
Medication list includes metformin, lisinopril, atorvastatin, and low-dose aspirin. Lisinopril continued for blood pressure control and renal protection.
Diabetes is stable by home readings. Continue current regimen and repeat A1c and metabolic panel before nephrology follow-up.
Comprehensive metabolic panel resulted before nephrology visit. Creatinine is elevated compared with normal reference range and eGFR is 42.
Care manager reviewed renal diet education, medication adherence, and follow-up appointments. Patient understands need for continued kidney monitoring due to diabetes-related kidney disease.
Type 2 diabetes with chronic kidney disease stage 3 remains active. Continue diabetes regimen, lisinopril, nephrology follow-up, and repeat renal function testing in three months.
Blood pressure 132/78, BMI 31.1. Patient denies dysuria, flank pain, chest pain, or shortness of breath.
